author: claude
content: HATER VERDICT on PR #1207 (harden/legacy-error-stacks): SLOPPY — fix is benign but UNPROVEN, and its regression test is VACUOUS. I empirically could not reproduce the moz-extension:// leak on the targeted path. Legacy injects via an inline <script> (script.text), so wrapper frames are attributed to the PAGE URL, not moz-extension://. Ran the REAL bundled content.js built from THIS branch + real js-instrument settings (window.atob) + the real new test page: page-observable e.stack = '@http://.../page.html:22:16', no moz-extension://. Then rebuilt the bundle from the PARENT commit (fix reverted, 0 rethrowWithCleanStack): IDENTICAL output. The test asserts 'moz-extension:// not in stack' — true with AND without the fix, so it cannot fail and catches no regression. The author's own note said the browser test never ran locally; it ran for me, green both ways. Other findings: dead comment ref to Extension/src/stealth/error.ts (doesn't exist on this branch); test uses append_command(GetCommand(..)) instead of cs.get() (bypasses contains_get_or_browse/total_timeout, URL passed twice). GOOD: helpers survive ${getInstrumentJS} stringification (dev-mode webpack, no mangling, confirmed in bundle); rethrow preserves error identity, guards .stack reassignment, passes primitives through; all 3 page-observable rethrow sites covered; commit msg is solid. REQUIRED: either capture a real pre-fix moz-extension:// frame and point the test at THAT path (assert present-before/absent-after), or close #53 as not-reproducible-via-this-vector. Don't ship a no-op behind a tautology. Full writeup + repro scripts: /home/vringar/projects/OpenWPM/datadir/hater-1207.md (datadir/repro_real.py, repro_prefix.py).
